{
  "name": "run-club-scraper-pipeline",
  "overview": "Build a periodic ingestion pipeline for local run clubs that combines website scraping with official Instagram API ingestion, then normalizes and deduplicates updates into a single reviewable feed.",
  "plan": "# Local Run Club Ingestion Pipeline Plan\n\n## Goal\nCreate a reliable, compliant pipeline that periodically checks club sources (websites + Instagram via official APIs), detects new/changed information, and outputs structured updates for review or downstream publishing.\n\n## Architecture (MVP-first)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart LR\n  sourceRegistry[SourceRegistry] --\u003e scheduler[Scheduler]\n  scheduler --\u003e webWorker[WebsiteIngestionWorker]\n  scheduler --\u003e igWorker[InstagramApiWorker]\n  webWorker --\u003e rawStore[RawSnapshotStore]\n  igWorker --\u003e rawStore\n  rawStore --\u003e normalizer[Normalizer]\n  normalizer --\u003e deduper[ChangeDetectorAndDeduper]\n  deduper --\u003e eventStore[ClubEventsAndUpdatesStore]\n  eventStore --\u003e reviewer[ReviewQueue]\n  reviewer --\u003e outputs[AlertsAndExports]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Phase 1: Source Registry + Data Model\n- Build a canonical source registry with one row per run club:\n  - &#96;club_id&#96;, &#96;club_name&#96;, &#96;source_type&#96; (&#96;website&#96; | &#96;instagram&#96;), &#96;source_url&#96;, &#96;poll_frequency&#96;, &#96;status&#96;, &#96;last_checked_at&#96;.\n- Define normalized entities:\n  - &#96;club_profile&#96; (name, location, contact links)\n  - &#96;run_event&#96; (date/time, location, distance, pace, recurring rule)\n  - &#96;update_event&#96; (what changed, source, timestamp, confidence).\n- Add a &#96;raw_snapshots&#96; table/bucket for raw HTML/API payloads + hash for traceability.\n\n## Phase 2: Website Ingestion Worker\n- Implement a website fetch worker that:\n  - pulls source URLs from registry,\n  - stores raw HTML snapshot,\n  - extracts candidate event/profile blocks using deterministic selectors + fallback text patterns.\n- Compute content hash per source and short-circuit unchanged pages.\n- Add per-domain backoff/retry and request throttling.\n\n## Phase 3: Instagram Official API Worker\n- Set up Meta app + required permissions for Instagram Business/Creator accounts where possible.\n- Implement API-based ingestion for profile metadata and recent media/captions.\n- Store raw API responses in &#96;raw_snapshots&#96;; normalize captions into event candidates.\n- Mark unsupported/private/non-permitted accounts as &#96;manual_review_required&#96; (not failed).\n\n## Phase 4: Normalization, Diffing, and Dedup\n- Normalize extracted content into &#96;run_event&#96; + &#96;club_profile&#96; schema.\n- Run change detection against latest known records:\n  - &#96;new_event&#96;, &#96;event_updated&#96;, &#96;event_cancelled&#96;, &#96;profile_changed&#96;.\n- Deduplicate by composite keys (&#96;club_id + datetime + location + title_similarity&#96;) and fuzzy title matching.\n\n## Phase 5: Scheduling + Operations\n- Schedule periodic checks (e.g., every 6h default; configurable per source).\n- Add worker health metrics:\n  - success rate, latency, extraction coverage, API quota usage.\n- Add alerting for repeated source failures and quota exhaustion.\n\n## Phase 6: Human-in-the-loop Review + Output\n- Create a review queue for low-confidence or ambiguous extractions.\n- Add outputs:\n  - daily digest (new/changed events),\n  - CSV/JSON export for your spreadsheet sync,\n  - optional webhook/Slack/email notification.\n\n## Compliance and Risk Guardrails\n- Instagram: API-only ingestion; no credential stuffing, no unauthorized scraping.\n- Respect website &#96;robots.txt&#96; intent and conservative rate limits.\n- Keep only necessary data; record source attribution and retrieval timestamp.\n- Log provenance (&#96;source_url&#96;, snapshot hash, parser version) for auditability.\n\n## Suggested MVP Scope (2 weeks)\n- 10-20 clubs total.\n- Website ingestion for all; Instagram API for eligible clubs.\n- One normalized output table + daily digest.\n- Manual review queue for unresolved sources.\n\n## Validation Checklist\n- At least 90% of known website event updates detected within one polling cycle.\n- Zero duplicate events in digest for a 7-day trial.\n- All Instagram-ingested records traceable to API payload IDs.\n- Clear failure reasons for every skipped source.\n\n## Implementation Notes\n- Start with deterministic rules before introducing LLM extraction; add LLM only for fallback parsing if needed.\n- Keep parsers source-specific where high-value clubs have stable patterns.\n- Version extraction logic to safely reprocess historical snapshots when parser improves.",

_**Agent (default)**_

Excellent call — that will likely get you to useful results much faster.

I updated the plan to a **hybrid pipeline**:
- **Automated:** websites
- **Manual but structured:** Instagram + Facebook review queue
- **Unified output:** same normalized event/update feed regardless of source

I changed the plan so social sources now flow through a dedicated `SocialManualReviewQueue` step with:
- review cadence fields (`manual_review_due_at`, `last_reviewed_at`)
- a standardized review checklist (next run info, update/cancellation, confidence, citation link)
- traceability rules so every social update is attributable

If you want, next I can refine this into a **very concrete weekly operating playbook** (who reviews what, how long it takes, and what “done” looks like per club), so it’s easy to run consistently.

_**Agent (default)**_

Implemented a working MVP pipeline for your hybrid process: **automated website checks + manual IG/Facebook review queue**.

### What I added

- New runnable module: `run_clubs_pipeline/`
  - `run_clubs_pipeline/pipeline.py`  
    - main runner command
    - seeds starter registry (`--seed`)
    - runs website checks
    - generates social review queue
    - updates registry timestamps
  - `run_clubs_pipeline/source_registry.py`  
    - CSV registry load/write + header management
  - `run_clubs_pipeline/website_worker.py`  
    - fetches website HTML
    - stores raw snapshots with hash
    - marks changed vs unchanged
    - writes website result CSV
  - `run_clubs_pipeline/manual_social_queue.py`  
    - creates due social review tasks for `instagram`/`facebook`
    - tier-based cadence defaults (`high` 48h, `medium` 96h, `low` 168h)
    - generates social capture template CSV
  - `run_clubs_pipeline/models.py` and `run_clubs_pipeline/__init__.py`

- Docs:
  - `RUN_CLUBS_PIPELINE.md` with usage + workflow
  - updated `README.md` with a pointer to the new pipeline

- Git hygiene:
  - updated `.gitignore` to ignore runtime artifacts in `data/run_clubs/`

### Verified working

I ran:

- `python3 run_clubs_pipeline/pipeline.py --seed`
- `python3 run_clubs_pipeline/pipeline.py`

Both completed successfully and produced:
- `data/run_clubs/source_registry.csv`
- `data/run_clubs/website_check_results.csv`
- `data/run_clubs/social_review_queue.csv`
- `data/run_clubs/social_review_capture.csv`
- `data/run_clubs/raw_snapshots/`

### Next best step

If you want, I can now do phase 2 and wire this directly to your spreadsheet input format (column mapping + import helper), so you can drop in your current club list and run immediately without manual CSV reshaping.

---

yes please.  do you want to see the current spreadsheet first?

Please share either:

If useful, include which columns are currently used for:
- club name
- website URL
- Instagram URL
- Facebook URL
- notes / status / priority

Once I see that, I’ll set up an import helper that converts it directly into `data/run_clubs/source_registry.csv` with minimal manual cleanup.

    source path/to/venv/bin/activate
    python3 -m pip install xyz
    
    If you wish to install a Python application that isn't in Homebrew,
    it may be easiest to use 'pipx install xyz', which will manage a
    virtual environment for you. You can install pipx with
    
    brew install pipx
    
    You may restore the old behavior of pip by passing
    the '--break-system-packages' flag to pip, or by adding
    'break-system-packages = true' to your pip.conf file. The latter
    will permanently disable this error.
    
    If you disable this error, we STRONGLY recommend that you additionally
    pass the '--user' flag to pip, or set 'user = true' in your pip.conf
    file. Failure to do this can result in a broken Homebrew installation.
    
    Read more about this behavior here: &lt;https://peps.python.org/pep-0668/&gt;

note: If you believe this is a mistake, please contact your Python installation or OS distribution provider. You can override this, at the risk of breaking your Python installation or OS, by passing --break-system-packages.

_**Agent (default)**_

Yes — and this was enough to wire it end-to-end.

I added an Excel importer and ran it against your file:
`/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/Data Files/Halifax_Running_Clubs_v5.xlsx`

### Implemented

- New importer: `run_clubs_pipeline/import_from_xlsx.py`
  - Reads your workbook/sheet
  - Maps one club row into 1..N sources (`website`, `instagram`, `facebook`)
  - Normalizes handle-style social values (e.g. `@clubname` -> full URL)
  - If `Website` contains an IG/FB URL, classifies it correctly as social
  - Writes to `data/run_clubs/source_registry.csv`
  - Supports `--replace-all` to fully rebuild registry from spreadsheet

- Docs updated:
  - `RUN_CLUBS_PIPELINE.md` (import command + behavior)
  - `README.md` (references importer)
- `.gitignore` updated to include `.venv/`

### Ran successfully

Importer command executed:

```bash
.venv/bin/python run_clubs_pipeline/import_from_xlsx.py \
  --xlsx "/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/Data Files/Halifax_Running_Clubs_v5.xlsx" \
  --sheet "Halifax Running Clubs" \
  --replace-all
```

Result:
- `Rows added: 30`
- Registry written to `data/run_clubs/source_registry.csv`

Then I ran the pipeline:
- `python3 run_clubs_pipeline/pipeline.py`
- Completed successfully and regenerated website/social outputs.

### Optional column additions (recommended but not required)

If you want better prioritization from sheet data, we can add:
- `Activity Tier` (`high|medium|low`)
- `Status` (`active|paused`)
- `Poll Frequency` (e.g. `6h`, `24h`, `weekly`)

I can add support for those columns next so your sheet fully drives scheduling behavior.

---

Done — and yes, that spreadsheet worked great.

I implemented the next step and added support for optional spreadsheet-driven controls.

### What I changed

- Restored/full scaffolded `run_clubs_pipeline` package (it had partially disappeared):
  - `run_clubs_pipeline/__init__.py`
  - `run_clubs_pipeline/models.py`
  - `run_clubs_pipeline/source_registry.py`
  - `run_clubs_pipeline/website_worker.py`
  - `run_clubs_pipeline/manual_social_queue.py`
  - `run_clubs_pipeline/pipeline.py`
  - `run_clubs_pipeline/import_from_xlsx.py` (existing, now enhanced)

- Enhanced Excel importer to support optional columns:
  - `Activity Tier` -> `activity_tier` (`high|medium|low`)
  - `Status` -> `status` (`active|paused`)
  - `Poll Frequency` -> `poll_frequency` (free text like `6h`, `24h`, `weekly`)
- Registry schema now includes `poll_frequency`.

- Updated docs:
  - `RUN_CLUBS_PIPELINE.md` now mentions these optional columns.
- Kept `.venv/` and `data/run_clubs/` ignored in `.gitignore`.

### Verification run

I re-ran everything using your workbook:

- Import:
  - `Rows added: 30`
- Pipeline:
  - completed successfully
  - regenerated `source_registry.csv`, `website_check_results.csv`, `social_review_queue.csv`, `social_review_capture.csv`, and snapshots.

### Next useful step

If you want, I can add a small **validation report command** that flags spreadsheet issues before import (missing URLs, duplicate clubs, malformed social handles, inactive rows with active cadence, etc.) so updates stay clean as the sheet evolves.

### 1) CSV import helper added

I added `run_clubs_pipeline/import_from_csv.py` so you can import directly from the cleaned CSV loop.

Usage:

```bash
.venv/bin/python run_clubs_pipeline/import_from_csv.py \
  --csv "data/run_clubs/cleaned_workbook_rows.csv" \
  --replace-all
```

I tested it successfully; it imported `30` rows into `data/run_clubs/source_registry.csv`.

---

### 2) Commit checkpoint created

I committed the full run-clubs MVP + importer/validator work.

- **Commit:** `085b237a`
- **Message:** `Add run-club ingestion MVP with spreadsheet import and validation.`

Includes:
- run-clubs pipeline package (`pipeline`, `website_worker`, `manual_social_queue`, etc.)
- spreadsheet import (`import_from_xlsx.py`)
- CSV import (`import_from_csv.py`)
- validation + report/autofix (`validate_xlsx.py`)
- docs (`RUN_CLUBS_PIPELINE.md`, `README.md`)

_**Agent (default)**_

Yes — quick local testing before push/PR is the right move.

Recommended order (5–10 min):

1. **Validate workbook**
   - `.venv/bin/python run_clubs_pipeline/validate_xlsx.py --xlsx "/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/Data Files/Halifax_Running_Clubs_v5.xlsx" --sheet "Halifax Running Clubs" --report-csv "data/run_clubs/validation_report.csv" --autofix-output-csv "data/run_clubs/cleaned_workbook_rows.csv"`

2. **Import from cleaned CSV**
   - `.venv/bin/python run_clubs_pipeline/import_from_csv.py --csv "data/run_clubs/cleaned_workbook_rows.csv" --replace-all`

3. **Run pipeline**
   - `python3 run_clubs_pipeline/pipeline.py`

4. **Spot-check outputs**
   - `data/run_clubs/source_registry.csv`
   - `data/run_clubs/website_check_results.csv`
   - `data/run_clubs/social_review_queue.csv`
   - `data/run_clubs/social_review_capture.csv`

What to confirm:
- no crash/errors,
- registry has expected club/source rows,
- social queue contains IG/FB items due for review,
- website checks show `ok`/`error` statuses that make sense.

I already did smoke runs while building, but you should still run this once end-to-end on your side before shipping.
